title: Dental Therapist
how_to_become: To become a dental therapist, you'll need to complete a pre-registration training programme and register with the General Dental Council (GDC).

salary.entry_note: Qualified dental therapists in the NHS start at £33,247 before tax.
salary.experienced_note: Specialist dental therapists can earn up to £50,702. Advanced dental therapists can earn up to £59,159 before tax.

description: Dental therapists play a crucial and expanding role within the dental team. They are qualified to diagnose dental diseases and provide a wide range of treatments within their scope of practice, including preventative advice, treatments for gum disease and tooth decay, restorative treatments, extractions, and crowns on children's primary teeth.
role_overview: As a dental therapist, you'll work within a team with an increasing focus on preventing disease and promoting healthy lifestyles. You may work in a general dental practice, community health centre, or hospital setting, providing direct care or treating patients under prescription from a dentist.

entry_requirements.overview: A pre-registration BSc degree in Oral Health Sciences approved by the GDC is typically required. English language proficiency is required by the GDC.
entry_requirements.alternative_entry: Some training providers accept applications from candidates who have successfully completed a SWAP access course.

work_experience: Work placements and volunteering can provide healthcare experience and help when applying to university, college, or a new job with NHSScotland.

education_and_training.degree_programme: BSc in Oral Health Sciences is a common pathway, approved by the GDC.
universities: University of Dundee — BSc Oral Health Sciences
Glasgow Caledonian University — BSc Oral Health Science
University of the Highlands and Islands — BSc Oral Health Science
education_and_training.application: Applications are typically made through UCAS. Entry requirements vary between providers.
education_and_training.english_language: All dental professionals must be sufficiently fluent in written and spoken English to communicate effectively with patients, relatives, the dental team, and other healthcare professionals.

learning_and_development.overview: Dental therapists must undertake ongoing continuing professional development (CPD) to maintain and update skills, knowledge, and competence and retain GDC registration.

extended_scope: Removing sutures after a wound has been checked by a dentist
Carrying out tooth whitening under the prescription of a dentist

professional_bodies: General Dental Council (GDC) — mandatory for NHS practice
British Society of Dental Hygiene and Therapy

chunks.text: To become a dental therapist, complete a pre-registration training programme and register with the General Dental Council (GDC).
Qualified dental therapists in the NHS start at £33,247 before tax. Specialist dental therapists can earn up to £50,702. Advanced dental therapists can earn up to £59,159 before tax.

Dental therapists diagnose dental diseases and provide a wide range of treatments including preventative advice, treatments for gum disease and tooth decay, fillings on adults' and children's teeth, extractions, and crowns on children's primary teeth.

Dental therapists work within a team in general dental practice, community health centres, or hospital settings, focusing on disease prevention and promoting healthy lifestyles. They provide direct care or treat patients under prescription from a dentist.

Useful school subjects include Biology or Human Biology, English, Maths, and Chemistry. A BSc in Oral Health Sciences approved by the GDC is typically required. SWAP access courses may be accepted by some providers. English language proficiency is required by the GDC.
Work placements and volunteering can provide healthcare experience and help when applying to university, college, or a new job with NHSScotland.

BSc Oral Health Sciences programmes are offered at the University of Dundee, Glasgow Caledonian University, and the University of the Highlands and Islands. Applications are typically made through UCAS.
Dental therapists must undertake ongoing CPD to maintain skills, knowledge, and competence and retain GDC registration.

Dental therapists can extend their scope to include inhalation sedation, removing sutures, and tooth whitening under prescription. They can also progress to management roles or become a dental therapist tutor.
Dental therapists must register with the General Dental Council (GDC). They can also join the British Association of Dental Therapists and the British Society of Dental Hygiene and Therapy.
